description: Wooded regions varying in density, age, and character.
regions: Woodlands
A broad canopy of mixed trees where dappled light reaches the mossy forest floor.
Thicket
A snarled tangle of saplings and bramble so dense that even deer must force their way through.
Grove
A small, quiet stand of trees set apart, often sacred or touched by old magic.
Copse
A handful of trees huddled together on open ground, offering brief shelter to passing travelers.
Wood
A modest stretch of forest, old enough to have trails but small enough to cross by noon.
Timberland
Tall, straight trunks rising in ordered ranks, prized by loggers and jealously guarded by foresters.
Wildwood
Untamed forest that has never known an axe, choked with undergrowth and ancient fallen giants.
Deepwood
Primeval forest where the canopy blots out the sky and roots older than memory grip the earth.
Greenwood
Lush, living forest thick with birdsong, fern, and the smell of rain on fresh leaves.
Darkwood
A gloomy woodland of close-set trunks and perpetual shadow, where silence presses like a weight.
Deadwood
A grey, skeletal forest of leafless trunks and ashen soil, killed by blight or cursed ground.
Thornwood
A cruel forest of barbed branches and razor-edged briars that draw blood at every step.
